objective: Add spawn/kill buttons to the boot-waterfall UI. Subprocess lifecycle owned by topology-ui process. All actions emit spans. Spawned subprocesses' boot-span chains appear in the waterfall view (closes the loop).
in_scope: POST /api/nodes/spawn { node_type } — forks subprocess with inherited OTEL_* env, tracks Child handle in DashMap
DELETE /api/nodes/{node_name} — SIGTERM (start_kill), 5s graceful wait, force-kill fallback, data dir cleanup
4 spawn buttons (gateway/broker/compute/registry) with green border styling + toast feedback
Kill button shown in controls row when currently-selected node is UI-spawned
In-page uiSpawned set tracks which node_names were spawned by this UI session
rafka.ui.subprocess.spawned + rafka.ui.subprocess.killed spans emitted with node_name/pid/reason tags
CARGO_TARGET_DIR env var documented in CLAUDE.md — topology-ui reads it to locate node binaries
E:/tmp/rafka-ui-nodes/{node_name}/ spawn dir created on spawn, cleaned on kill
out_of_scope: Persistent data dirs across UI restarts
exit_criteria: POST /api/nodes/spawn returns 201 with node_name + pid — DONE (broker-cef5a8bf pid=77084)
Spawned subprocess boot trace appears in Jaeger (OTEL env propagation) — DONE (5 rafka spans)
DELETE /api/nodes/{name} returns 200 with reason=graceful|forced — DONE (graceful in ~1s)
Subprocess process gone after kill — DONE (pid 77084 gone, verified via Get-Process)
Data dir E:/tmp/rafka-ui-nodes/{name}/ deleted after kill — DONE (Test-Path returns False)
rafka.ui.subprocess.killed in Jaeger with node_name + reason tags — DONE (count=1, graceful)
Kill button visible in UI when UI-spawned node is selected — DONE (Option A: controls row)
